description: Win98 behavioral denylist — symbols that are present in win98se-api-allowlist.json (i.e. exist as exports in Win98 SE's real DLLs) BUT are non-functional at runtime on Win98. Static imports of these bind successfully at PE load time and then fail at runtime, often silently. The verifier rejects them so consumers must either patch out the call or take an explicit fallback path.
rationale: The allowlist is generated from a real Win98 SE install and reflects the export table verbatim. Many NT-era APIs (especially the entire security/SID/ACL/Token machinery in advapi32) appear there as stubs that always return failure on the Win9x kernel. Generated data should stay faithful to its source, so we keep the allowlist clean and supplement it with this hand-curated behavioral policy.
generated_from: advapi32 entries: every symbol in Win98 SE's advapi32.dll export table whose name matches the NT-security-API prefix set (Open*Token, Get/SetTokenInformation, *Sid, *Acl, *SecurityDescriptor, *SecurityInfo, Logon*, Lookup*, Impersonate*, Adjust*, RevertToSelf, CheckTokenMembership, PrivilegeCheck, MakeAbsolute/SelfRelativeSD, BuildExplicitAccess*, BuildTrusteeWith*, ...). All confirmed present in win98se-api-allowlist.json but non-functional on the Win9x kernel.
